name: Fort Peck Community
description_short: Fort Peck Community College is a regionally accredited two-year college in northeast Montana offering associate degrees, certificates, and transfer-friendly programs with online learning options.
description_long:
Fort Peck Community College (FPCC) is a regionally accredited community college located in Poplar, Montana, serving the Fort Peck region and northeast Montana. Founded to provide accessible higher education to the local community, FPCC operates as an educational institution rather than a financial services provider. The college offers multiple pathways to credential completion, including Associate of Arts degrees for transfer students, Associate of Science degrees for STEM careers, and Associate of Applied Science degrees designed for immediate employment. Additionally, FPCC provides one-year Certificate of Applied Science programs and early college dual-credit opportunities for high school juniors and seniors.

FPCC's core offerings center on academic programming with flexibility for diverse student needs. The institution provides regionally accredited degrees that transfer to four-year universities, eliminating barriers for students intending to pursue bachelor's degrees. Online learning is available across programs, enabling access for students unable to attend campus in person. The college explicitly welcomes all students regardless of tribal affiliation, though it serves the Fort Peck Assiniboine and Sioux Tribes as an anchor institution. Support services include online tutoring, canvas learning management system access, and academic advising through their My FPCC (JICS) portal.

FPCC distinguishes itself through strong community integration and cultural responsiveness. As a tribal-serving institution located on or near Fort Peck Reservation land, the college maintains direct partnership with the Fort Peck Assiniboine and Sioux Tribes. The institution emphasizes "the power of connection, culture, and community" in its mission statement, positioning education as culturally grounded. FPCC also offers early college programs allowing high school students to earn college credits before graduation, reducing barriers to degree completion.

It is important to note that Fort Peck Community College is an educational institution and not a consumer finance company. This listing appears to result from miscategorization in the CreditDoc database. FPCC provides no payday loans, personal loans, credit repair, debt relief, or financial products of any kind. The college operates as a nonprofit educational entity accredited by the Northwest Commission on Colleges and Universities. Students and community members seeking financial products or services should not contact FPCC for those purposes.
services: Associate of Applied Science degree programs
Certificate of Applied Science one-year programs
Early college dual-credit high school courses
My FPCC (JICS) student portal and advising
Campus incident and Title IX reporting
pros: Regionally accredited by NWCCU, ensuring credits transfer to four-year institutions
Online learning available, enabling access for remote or working students
No tribal affiliation requirement; explicitly welcomes all students
Early college dual-credit program allows high school juniors/seniors to earn college credit
Low cost community college model provides affordable pathway to degree completion
Multiple credential pathways (Associate of Arts, Science, Applied Science, Certificates)
Located in underserved northeast Montana, providing local educational access
cons: Not a financial services provider; miscategorized in payday-alternatives database
Limited geographic reach serving primarily northeast Montana region
No information on website regarding financial aid, grants, or loan resources for students
Website lacks detail on tuition costs, program-specific requirements, or enrollment processes
No consumer finance services, personal loans, or emergency funding available
best_for: High school graduates in northeast Montana seeking affordable associate degrees
Students intending to transfer to four-year universities for bachelor's degrees
Working adults and place-bound students needing flexible online education options
Fort Peck Tribes members and community members seeking credential programs
diagnosis: Fort Peck Community College is an educational institution, not a consumer finance company, and has been miscategorized in the payday-alternatives category. This listing should be removed or recategorized as a banking/educational institution. Users seeking financial products, loans, credit services, or emergency cash should not contact FPCC.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
